lede: A look at July 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Connor Bedard and Leif Hoste, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2015 Khan Bani Saad bombing
events.desc: At least 120 people are killed and 130 injured by a suicide bombing in Diyala Governorate, Iraq.

events.title: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17
events.desc: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, a Boeing 777, crashes near the border of Ukraine and Russia after being shot down. All 298 people on board are killed.

events.title: TAM Airlines Flight 3054
events.desc: TAM Airlines Flight 3054, an Airbus A320, crashes into a warehouse after landing too fast and missing the end of the São Paulo–Congonhas Airport runway, killing 199 people.

events.title: TWA Flight 800
events.desc: TWA Flight 800: Off the coast of Long Island, New York, a Paris-bound TWA Boeing 747 explodes, killing all 230 on board.

events.title: Brazil national football team
events.desc: Brazil win their fourth World Cup title, defeating Italy 3–2 on penalties.

events.title: Mohammad Zahir Shah
events.desc: King Mohammed Zahir Shah of Afghanistan, while having surgery in Italy, is deposed by his cousin Mohammed Daoud Khan.

events.title: Port Chicago disaster
events.desc: Port Chicago disaster: Near the San Francisco Bay, two ships laden with ammunition for the war explode in Port Chicago, California, killing 320.

events.title: RMS Carpathia
events.desc: The RMS Carpathia is sunk off Ireland by the German SM U-55; five lives are lost.

events.title: Vega
events.desc: Vega becomes the first star (other than the Sun) to be photographed.

events.title: Battle of Castillon
events.desc: Battle of Castillon: The last battle of the Hundred Years' War, the French under Jean Bureau defeat the English under the Earl of Shrewsbury, who is killed in the battle in Gascony.

births.name: Edward Natapei
births.line: Vanuatuan politician, 6th Prime Minister of Vanuatu (died 2015)

deaths.text: TAM Airlines Flight 3054, Brazilian lawyer and businessman
David Hogan (composer), American composer and musical director
Rasmus Rasmussen (actor), Norwegian actor, singer, and director
Charles Grey, 2nd Earl Grey, English politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alexius of Rome (Western Church)
